fundamentals.name: Datadog, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Datadog, Inc. operates an observability and security platform for cloud applications in the United States and internationally. The company's products comprise infrastructure and application performance monitoring, log management, observability pipelines, synthetics, real user monitoring, product analytics, continuous profiler, database monitoring, data observability, LLM observability, error tracking, network monitoring, incident response, workflow automation and App builder, event management, bits AI SRE, cloud cost management, cloud security, code security, cloud SIEM, threat management, sensitive data scanner, and CI visibility. Datadog, Inc. was incorporated in 2010 and is headquartered in New York, New York.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing software company with high margins but a very high valuation and low operating profitability.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.3%; next earnings date is 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 32.2% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 113.4% indicate strong top-line and bottom-line expansion.
Gross margin of 79.9% is best-in-class, suggesting strong pricing power for its software.
Strong free cash flow of $936.73M provides ample liquidity.
Analyst consensus is 'strong buy' with a mean target price suggesting modest upside.
Current ratio of 3.40 and quick ratio of 3.29 indicate excellent short-term liquidity.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 566.36 and Forward P/E of 77.65 are extremely high, indicating a very expensive stock.
Operating margin of 0.8% and profit margin of 3.7% are surprisingly low given the high gross margin, suggesting high operating expenses.
Return on equity of 3.9% and return on assets of -0.2% are weak.
Debt/Equity ratio of 32.22 is high, although offset by substantial cash reserves.

news.paragraph: Datadog (DDOG) has seen significant positive attention recently, hitting a 52-week high and being highlighted as a trending stock with strong analyst buy ratings. The company's valuation has surged, with its share price increasing 64% in the past month, and it is benefiting from the broader trend of cybersecurity stocks reaching record levels. With positive analyst views and a strong recent earnings surprise, the stock appears poised for continued momentum, though its high valuation warrants consideration.
